page: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv6 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

This server is bundled with 2835 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The inclusion of substantial local storage makes it suitable for applications requiring fast local scratch space or temporary database storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance across multiple domains. In memory bandwidth tests, the instance achieved 232.42 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 100.8 GB/sec for large block write operations, both placing in the strong category. Computational benchmarks show a Geekbench multi-core score of 19924.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 62410.33. Image processing performance is particularly notable, scoring 20232.0 in Geekbench, which falls into the top tier category. For database operations, the server processed 67.91 million Redis SET operations per second and scored 25573.3 in Passmark database tests.

The hardware's support for Intel AMX is reflected in its LLM inference benchmarks. It achieved a top-tier text generation speed of 342.39 tokens/sec for a small 135M model, though performance scales down to an average of 3.6 tokens/sec for a large 70B model. Given these characteristics, the D48ds_v6 is suited for relational databases, Redis caching layers, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate-scale machine learning inference workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv6 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) and 192.0 GB of RAM, maintaining a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance includes 2835 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, but lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmarks demonstrate strong capabilities, including a Passmark CPU score of 62410.33, 67.91 million Redis SET operations per second, and top-tier image processing performance. It also exhibits strong memory bandwidth, reaching 232.42 GB/sec for large block reads. Supported by Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets, this server is optimized for database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, delivering strong database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v6 is a 48-vCPU general-purpose VM with 192 GB RAM, 2835 GB local storage, and strong database benchmark performance.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed local storage, and advanced matrix extension capabilities.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8573C at 4.2 GHz.
2835 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations.
Strong memory bandwidth and database performance, including 67.91 million Redis SET operations per second.
Suited for database hosting, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference.
